skill.name: Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs.Extensions.AuthenticationEvents (.NET)
skill.description: Azure Functions extension for handling Microsoft Entra ID custom authentication events including token enrichment, custom claims, attribute collection, and OTP customization.
skill.summary: Handle Microsoft Entra ID authentication events in Azure Functions with custom claims, attribute collection, and OTP delivery.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only documentation skill for Microsoft Azure WebJobs Authentication Events SDK. No executable code detected. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 security findings. This skill provides reference documentation and code examples for implementing Azure Functions triggers for Entra ID authentication events including token issuance, attribute collection, and OTP delivery. Safe to publish as educational content.

content.user_title: Build Azure Functions for Entra ID Authentication Events
content.value_statement: Implement custom authentication extensions for Microsoft Entra ID with Azure Functions. Add custom claims to tokens, validate user attributes during sign-up, and deliver OTP codes through custom channels.
actual_capabilities: Generate Azure Functions code for handling OnTokenIssuanceStart events with custom claims
Create attribute collection handlers for prefilling form values and validating user submissions
Implement custom OTP delivery via SMS, email, or push notifications
Configure Function App settings for authentication events integration
Design error handling patterns for authentication event timeouts
Structure Entra ID custom authentication extensions and app registrations
limitations: Cannot directly access or modify Entra ID configuration - provides code examples only
Requires Azure Functions hosting environment and Entra ID tenant setup
Does not include SMS provider implementations - must integrate your own Twilio or Azure Communication Services
Cannot execute or test authentication events - only generates code structure
Limited to .NET Azure Functions (isolated worker model)

use_cases.title: Token Enrichment for Authorization
use_cases.description: Add custom claims like department, employee ID, and roles to access tokens during user sign-in. Use these claims in downstream APIs for authorization decisions without additional database lookups.

use_cases.title: Custom User Registration Validation
use_cases.description: Validate user attribute submissions during self-service sign-up flows. Block registrations from certain email domains, enforce password complexity, or require additional verification steps before account creation.

use_cases.title: Custom OTP Delivery Channels
use_cases.description: Replace default Microsoft OTP delivery with custom SMS providers, email services, or push notifications. Implement custom formatting, rate limiting, and delivery tracking for multi-factor authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Token Claims Function
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Azure Function that adds custom claims to tokens during Entra ID sign-in. Include claims for department, employeeId, and costCenter.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs basic token enrichment for authorization

prompt_templates.title: Attribute Collection Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an Azure Function to validate attribute collection submissions. Block sign-ups from temporary email domains and ensure display name is at least 3 characters.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer implementing user registration validation rules

prompt_templates.title: External Data Token Enrichment
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a token issuance function that fetches user roles from an external API and adds them as claims. Include error handling for API failures and caching logic.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer integrating external HR system with Entra ID

prompt_templates.title: Custom OTP with Twilio Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement an OTP send event handler using Twilio for SMS delivery. Include error handling, logging, and response actions for success and failure scenarios.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer building custom MFA delivery system

output_examples.input: Create a function to add department and employeeId claims during token issuance
output_examples.output: Generated WebJobsTokenIssuanceStartResponse with WebJobsProvideClaimsForToken action containing department and employeeId dictionary claims, including logging for user ID and correlation ID tracking.

output_examples.input: Write attribute collection validation that blocks temporary email domains
output_examples.output: Generated WebJobsAttributeCollectionSubmitResponse with domain validation logic, WebJobsShowBlockPage action for blocked domains, and WebJobsModifyAttributeValues for valid submissions with trimmed display names.
Generated WebJobsOnOtpSendResponse with SendSmsAsync integration, WebJobsOnOtpSendSuccess action on successful delivery, and WebJobsOnOtpSendFailed action with error details on exceptions.

best_practices: Always validate request data before processing - never trust that userId or OTP values are present
Return empty response objects instead of throwing exceptions to avoid blocking authentication flow
Log correlation IDs from AuthenticationContext for troubleshooting failed authentication events
Cache external API responses to avoid timeout limits on authentication event handlers
anti_patterns: Do not throw exceptions in authentication event handlers - return empty response to allow authentication to continue
Avoid blocking the main thread with slow external API calls - use async patterns and implement caching
Never hardcode API keys or connection strings - use Azure Key Vault or managed identity for secure access

faq.question: What happens if my authentication event function throws an exception?
faq.answer: The authentication flow may fail completely. Always wrap your logic in try-catch blocks and return an empty response object on error to allow authentication to continue without your custom logic.

faq.question: Can I modify claims after they have been added to the token?
faq.answer: No. Claims can only be added during the OnTokenIssuanceStart event before the token is issued. Once issued, the token claims are immutable until the next authentication event.

faq.question: What is the timeout limit for authentication event functions?
faq.answer: Authentication events must complete quickly, typically within a few seconds. Long-running operations like database queries should use caching or async patterns to avoid timeouts.

faq.question: Do I need to register an App Registration for custom authentication extensions?
faq.answer: Yes. You must register an application in Entra ID, expose an API with the correct scope (CustomAuthenticationExtension.Receive.Payload), and configure the custom authentication extension to point to your Function App URL.

faq.question: Can I use this SDK with the in-process Azure Functions model?
faq.answer: The examples shown are for the isolated worker model. The SDK also supports in-process but requires different initialization patterns in the Startup class.

faq.question: How do I test authentication event functions locally?
faq.answer: Use Azure Functions Core Tools to run locally, then send sample JSON payloads matching WebJobsTokenIssuanceStartRequest or WebJobsAttributeCollectionStartRequest structures via HTTP or test the function methods directly with unit tests.
